groups.name: TV shows fan campaigns brought back from cancellation
groups.explanation: Firefly (cancelled by Fox; fan campaigns led to the Serenity film continuation), Jericho (cancelled by CBS; fans mailed nuts to the network and got it renewed for a second season), Veronica Mars (cancelled by UPN/CW; a fan-funded Kickstarter movie followed, then a Hulu revival), and Wentworth (the Australian series faced network axe threats multiple times and fan pressure sustained it through to a conclusion) were all meaningfully brought back or continued through sustained fan pressure after cancellation.

groups.name: Characters operating under a false identity to pursue a personal vendetta
groups.explanation: Sydney Bristow (Alias) perpetually operates under constructed cover identities while pursuing enemies of those she loves; Emily Thorne (Revenge) is actually Amanda Clarke, concealing her real name to destroy the Graysons who framed her father; Patrick Jane (The Mentalist) hides behind a consultant role while secretly conducting a years-long personal vendetta against serial killer Red John; Claire Underwood (House of Cards) relentlessly pursues power and revenge while projecting a carefully constructed public persona that conceals her true ruthless motives.

groups.name: Actors who starred in a prestige TV drama AND voiced a character in a video game
groups.explanation: Carla Gugino (known for Haunting of Hill House) voiced Pamela Isley in Batman: Arkham Knight; Michael C. Hall (Dexter) voiced David Sarif in Deus Ex: Human Revolution; Anna Torv (Fringe) voiced Tali'Zorah in the Mass Effect trilogy; Hugh Dancy (Hannibal) voiced characters in video game productions. Each is primarily known for a landmark prestige drama role but also did significant voice performance work in major video games.

groups.name: Sounds like a spooky TV location — but it is actually a character name
groups.explanation: Patience, Mississippi sounds like an atmospheric small-town show setting but is actually the name of a character in The CW's Walker; Melisandre sounds like it could be a gothic European village or estate but is actually the Red Priestess in Game of Thrones; The Barn sounds like a rural horror location but is actually the name of a mysterious recurring entity or dimensional construct in Haven; Erica Kane sounds like it could be the name of a remote inn or gothic manor but is in fact the iconic central character of the long-running soap All My Children, played by Susan Lucci.
